cards.title: OpenAI Expands ChatGPT for Family Users
cards.body: OpenAI is hiring a dedicated product manager to build ChatGPT experiences for families, caregivers, and older adults. The move signals the company's push to deepen household penetration beyond individual users.

cards.title: Meta Removes Controversial Instagram AI Feature
cards.body: Meta has discontinued its AI image referencing feature on Instagram after user backlash. The company acknowledged the feature "missed the mark" despite intentions to provide creative control.

cards.title: Apple Sues OpenAI Over Trade Secrets
cards.body: Apple has filed a lawsuit alleging OpenAI engaged in misconduct directed by senior leadership, including a former Apple employee. The dispute involves alleged trade secret theft.

cards.title: Open Source AI Boom Accelerates Growth
cards.body: Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue reports open source AI is thriving, with the platform now used by roughly half the Fortune 500. The company has become a GitHub-equivalent for AI model and dataset sharing.

cards.title: SK Hynix Closes Record Foreign IPO
cards.body: SK Hynix raised $26.5 billion in the largest foreign IPO in U.S. history, marking the AI chip boom's biggest Wall Street moment. U.S. officials are urging the company to build domestic manufacturing facilities.

cards.title: OpenAI's GPT-5.6 Powers Microsoft Copilot
cards.body: OpenAI announced GPT-5.6 will be the preferred model for Microsoft Copilot 365 across workplace and productivity apps. The partnership underscores continued collaboration despite IPO speculation.

cards.title: OpenAI's No. 2 Executive Steps Down
cards.body: Fidji Simo is stepping down from her full-time role as OpenAI's No. 2 after extended medical leave. The departure creates leadership uncertainty as the company pursues an IPO and competes with Anthropic.

cards.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.6 Model Family
cards.body: OpenAI released its new GPT-5.6 family of models with improvements across cybersecurity and other domains. The launch strengthens the company's competitive position in the enterprise market.

cards.title: AI Agent Startup Raises $100M Autonomously
cards.body: Lyzr, an enterprise AI agent startup, used its own AI agent to raise a $100 million funding round. The self-directed fundraise demonstrates the practical capabilities of agentic AI systems.

cards.title: OpenAI Shuts Down Atlas Browser
cards.body: OpenAI is discontinuing Atlas, its AI-powered browser, less than a year after launch. The company is migrating agentic browsing features to its desktop app and Chrome extension instead.

cards.title: Musk Pledges Support for Anthropic Models
cards.body: Elon Musk praised Mythos and Fable, promising not to restrict Anthropic's model hosting. The reassurance addresses concerns about leveraging his infrastructure for competing AI companies.

cards.title: Meta Launches Muse Spark AI Coding Tool
cards.body: Meta entered the AI coding space with Muse Spark 1.1, focusing on handling large agentic workloads and code migrations. The tool targets enterprises increasingly relying on AI for automation.

cards.title: New York Times Alleges OpenAI Evidence Hidden
cards.body: The New York Times says OpenAI concealed tools and datasets identifying copyrighted journalism in ChatGPT outputs. The accusation escalates the copyright lawsuit with a new motion for sanctions.

cards.title: Google Requires AI Ad Disclosure Labels
cards.body: Google will now mandate disclosure when ads contain AI-generated or digitally altered content. The policy expands previous election-only disclosure requirements across all ad categories.

cards.title: Gradium Raises $100M Backed by Nvidia
cards.body: Paris-based AI voice startup Gradium closed a $100 million seed round with Nvidia backing. The funding will support a new Bay Area office to compete for top AI talent.

cards.title: Government Safety Review Process Remains Opaque
cards.body: Details about how government evaluated OpenAI's frontier models for safe release remain unclear. The regulatory approval process between authorities and AI companies lacks public transparency.

cards.title: Instagram Users Can Opt Out of AI Training
cards.body: Instagram users can now prevent Meta's AI from using their public photos for image generation. The control addresses privacy concerns following the Muse Image feature backlash.

cards.title: Meta's Custom AI Chips Begin Production
cards.body: Meta's internally-designed AI chips will enter production in September with a modular architecture. The flexible design anticipates evolving needs as AI technology rapidly advances.

cards.title: OpenAI Targets Families With ChatGPT Product
cards.body: OpenAI is hiring a dedicated product manager to build ChatGPT experiences for families, caregivers, and older adults. The move signals the company's expansion beyond enterprise and individual users.

cards.title: Meta Removes AI Feature From Instagram
cards.body: Meta has discontinued a controversial AI feature that generated images using public Instagram photos after user backlash. The company acknowledged the feature missed the mark despite good intentions.

cards.title: Apple Sues OpenAI Over Trade Secrets
cards.body: Apple alleges OpenAI engaged in misconduct directed by senior leadership, including a former employee. The lawsuit represents a rare legal confrontation between major AI players.

cards.title: Hugging Face: Open Source AI is Booming
cards.body: Hugging Face has become the GitHub for AI, enabling builders to share and download open models and datasets. The platform is now used by roughly half the Fortune 500.

cards.title: SK Hynix IPO Capitalizes on AI Chip Boom
cards.body: SK Hynix raised $26.5B in the biggest foreign IPO in US history, fueled by AI demand. The company is now being urged to build new U.S. semiconductor manufacturing facilities.

cards.title: GPT 5.6 Powers Microsoft Copilot 365
cards.body: OpenAI announced GPT 5.6 as the preferred model for Microsoft's Copilot 365 productivity suite. The partnership deepens integration of OpenAI's latest models into enterprise workflows.

cards.title: OpenAI's No. 2 Executive Steps Down
cards.body: Fidji Simo is stepping down from her full-time role as OpenAI's No. 2 after extended medical leave. The departure creates a leadership gap as OpenAI pursues an IPO and competes with Anthropic.

cards.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.6 Model Family
cards.body: OpenAI's latest model family delivers improvements across multiple areas including cybersecurity and enterprise applications. The release marks another step in the company's rapid iteration cycle.

cards.title: AI Agent Startup Raises $100M Using AI
cards.body: Lyzr used its own AI agent to run a $100 million fundraising round, demonstrating the technology's real-world effectiveness. The achievement validates the startup's core product offering.

cards.title: OpenAI Shuts Down AI Browser Atlas
cards.body: OpenAI is sunsetting its AI-powered browser after less than a year but will integrate agentic browsing features into its desktop app and Chrome extension. The pivot reflects refined product strategy.

cards.title: Elon Musk Pledges Support for Anthropic
cards.body: Musk promised not to cut off Anthropic if it hosts models on his infrastructure, addressing trust concerns. With $40 billion in revenue at stake, the commitment matters significantly.

cards.title: AI ROI Debate Reaches $3 Trillion
cards.body: The discussion around AI return on investment has intensified with stakes now in the trillions. Questions persist about whether AI investments will deliver promised returns.

cards.title: Meta Launches Muse Spark 1.1 AI Coder
cards.body: Meta's AI coding tool Muse Spark 1.1 handles large agentic workloads, bug fixes, and code migrations. The release positions Meta as a competitor in the crowded AI coding market.

cards.title: NYT Alleges OpenAI Hid Evidence in Trial
cards.body: News publishers claim OpenAI concealed tools and datasets that could identify copyrighted journalism in ChatGPT. The motion for sanctions escalates the ongoing copyright dispute.

cards.title: Google Requires AI Disclosure in Ads
cards.body: Google will now require advertisers to disclose when ads use AI-generated or digitally altered content. The policy extends existing transparency rules for election ads to all synthetic media.

cards.title: Paris AI Voice Startup Gradium Raises $100M
cards.body: Gradium, backed by Nvidia, raised $100 million in seed funding to expand voice AI capabilities. The company plans to open a Bay Area office to compete for top talent.

cards.title: Government Oversight of AI Model Releases Unclear
cards.body: Details remain murky about how government regulators evaluated OpenAI's frontier models for safety. The lack of transparency raises questions about AI governance processes.

cards.title: Stop Meta's AI From Using Your Photos
cards.body: Meta's Muse Image tool generates AI pictures using photos from public Instagram accounts without explicit consent. Users can adjust privacy settings to prevent their images from being used.

cards.title: Meta's Custom AI Chips Launch in September
cards.body: Meta is adopting a modular chip design approach to accommodate rapid AI evolution. Production begins in September as the company reduces dependence on external chip suppliers.

cards.title: Anthropic Reveals Claude's Inner Workings
cards.body: Anthropic developed a tool that provides unprecedented insight into how Claude processes concepts and answers questions. The findings range from mundane to unsettling about large language model reasoning.
